subject: REL
course_description: This course will explore classic religious texts in depth, developing skills to interpret sacred, philosophical and ethical works. Social, cultural, and/or philosophical contexts at work will provide interpretive frameworks.
course_notes: This course satisfies the Religion major requirement:- Tradition-Other. This course explores the ideas of Daoism (Taoism), one of the major philosophical traditions of ancient China. We will be thinking through what the texts meant in their original context, and what they might mean for us today. We begin with a close reading of the Daodejing (Tao Te Ching) by Laozi (Lao Tzu), the principal classic text. Themes include: ideas about language, political philosophy, relativism, the cultivation of the body, desire, action, and cosmology. We will then read selections from the Daoist text Zhuangzi (Chuang Tzu), and a brief selection of later Taoist works. We will then focus on two themes: what these Taoist texts have to say about war and violence, and about the ideal peaceful society. We will discover that "going with the flow" is never a simple matter.
